turns.text: Hi, I'm the owner of three coffee shops in Austin.
Honestly we already use spreadsheets and it works fine.
Okay. And what does it cost?
Yeah hi, I manage a single espresso bar downtown.
That sounds way too expensive for a small shop like ours.
Fine, how long does the trial last?
I run the roastery and the cafe, so I'm slammed.
We just don't have time to set up new software right now.
An afternoon? Really? What's involved exactly?
We're on Square right now for everything.
Does it pull the sales data in automatically or is that manual?
Nice, okay. And when could we start?
Quick question, can it also run payroll for my baristas?
Ah shame. What does it actually do then?
Got it, inventory only. That's still useful.
Our biggest problem is honestly milk waste, we pour so much away.
How would software even help with that?
Interesting. What would that cost for two locations?
I'm the shift manager, the owner isn't in today.
I can't really sign off on anything myself, it's not my call.
Sure, send the details and I'll pass them along.
We're opening a second location in September.
So we'd want everything running before the fall rush.
Great, let's book that demo for Thursday then.
We looked at MarketMan last year and hated the setup.
What makes yours any different from those big systems?
Hm. Fourteen days free, you said?
Give me the pricing straight, no fluff please.
Is that per location or total for all five of my shops?
Alright, that's actually reasonable for five.
